verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase that is N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane. Its SH2, SH3, and kinase domains all face the cytosol; there is no extracellular domain or transmembrane segment. It is not GPI-anchored, not secreted, and not a TM partner in a co-trafficked surface complex. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer/stress outer-leaflet ecto-pool; no cell-death-induced surface exposure documented for LYN specifically; no germline/tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display; not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool on the outer face; no stable TM-partner tethering. No CD designation. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic programs target the LYN protein body extracellularly. Inner-leaflet anchoring is the definitive classification.
